# Error Handling Checklist: Unified landing page and IA shell

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-002 error-handling requirements for Astro/Starlight hard blockers, fixable configuration errors, fallback order, docs-site command failures, link validation, and GitHub Pages base/path issues.
**Created**: 2026-06-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Framework Blockers And Fallbacks

- [x] CHK001 Are true Astro/Starlight hard blockers distinguished from ordinary repository configuration, package-script, content, route, or Pages path errors? [Resolved, Spec FR-015, Spec Assumptions, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Research Keep Astro with Starlight]
- [x] CHK002 Is the fallback order explicit as Docusaurus/MDX, then VitePress, then repo-native Markdown if Astro/Starlight cannot satisfy a hard blocker? [Resolved, Research Keep Astro with Starlight, DOC-001 decision record]
- [x] CHK003 Are the blocker categories narrow enough to avoid reopening framework selection for ordinary config failures? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Research local deterministic gate decision]
- [x] CHK004 Does the contract expose a machine-readable framework blocker boundary and fallback order for implementation/review tools? [Resolved, Contract errorHandling.frameworkHardBlockers, Contract errorHandling.fallbackOrder]

## Package Manager And Setup Failures

- [x] CHK005 Are docs-site command roles scoped to `pnpm` from `docs-site/` rather than root plugin test commands? [Resolved, Spec FR-002, Plan Selected Command Roles, Data Model Validation Command Role, Contract commands]
- [x] CHK006 Does the quickstart or plan tell maintainers what to do when `pnpm` is missing before dependency setup? [Resolved, Spec FR-023, Spec Assumptions, Plan Error Handling Next Actions, Quickstart Prerequisites, Contract nextActions.missingPnpm]
- [x] CHK007 Are dependency install and lockfile refresh treated as setup that may require package-registry access, not the repeatable DOC-002 completion gate? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Quickstart Setup]

## Build Failure Handling

- [x] CHK008 Are production build failure classes documented for Astro/Starlight config, content/frontmatter/schema, route generation, sidebar slugs, and Pages config? [Resolved, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Data Model Validation Command Role, Contract failureModes]
- [x] CHK009 Do build failure requirements give concrete next actions for maintainers without treating fixable config/content errors as framework-selection blockers? [Resolved, Spec FR-023, Plan Error Handling Next Actions, Quickstart Production Build, Contract nextActions.buildFailure]
- [x] CHK010 Does the minimum gate avoid deployment, browser screenshots, analytics checks, external-link crawling, and remote official-doc URL availability? [Resolved, Spec FR-022, Research local deterministic gate decision, Contract minimumCompletionGate]

## Link Validation Failure Handling

- [x] CHK011 Are internal-link validation failures scoped to Markdown/MDX routes, anchors, trailing slashes, and same-site/base-path issues? [Resolved, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Data Model Validation Command Role, Contract failureModes]
- [x] CHK012 Do link-validation failure requirements give concrete next actions for fixing route links, anchors, trailing slashes, and base-path assumptions? [Resolved, Spec FR-023, Plan Error Handling Next Actions, Quickstart Internal-Link Validation, Contract nextActions.linkValidationFailure]
- [x] CHK013 Is external-link crawling explicitly excluded from DOC-002's minimum completion gate and deferred to DOC-010? [Resolved, Spec FR-022, Research local deterministic gate decision, Contract deferredToDoc010]

## GitHub Pages Base/Path Failures

- [x] CHK014 Are Pages-ready values stated concretely as `site: "https://racecraft-lab.github.io"`, `base: "/racecraft-plugins-public"`, and `trailingSlash: "always"`? [Resolved, Plan Phase 0 Decisions, Data Model Documentation Site, Contract pagesConfig]
- [x] CHK015 Does the artifact set tell maintainers how to resolve Pages base/path mismatch failures while preserving the no-publish-workflow boundary? [Resolved, Spec FR-023, Plan Error Handling Next Actions, Quickstart Full DOC-002 Validation, Contract nextActions.pagesMismatch]
- [x] CHK016 Is the no-publish-workflow rule treated as an error-handling boundary rather than a missing implementation step? [Resolved, Spec FR-014, Plan Phase 0 Decisions, Research Pages decision, Data Model Documentation Site]

## Verification Run 2

- [x] CHK021 Does the route-shell contract now expose hard blockers, fixable non-blockers, fallback order, and next actions for setup/build/link/Pages failures? [Resolved, Contract errorHandling]
- [x] CHK022 Do the spec and plan now require missing-`pnpm`, build, link-validation, Pages mismatch, and hard-blocker dispositions with objective next actions? [Resolved, Spec FR-023, Spec SC-012, Plan Error Handling Next Actions]
- [x] CHK023 Does the quickstart now tell maintainers what to do after missing package manager, install/setup, production build, internal-link validation, and Pages mismatch failures? [Resolved, Quickstart Prerequisites, Setup, Production Build, Internal-Link Validation, Full DOC-002 Validation]
- [x] CHK024 Does the data model now define Error Handling Disposition rules without expanding DOC-002 into DOC-010 deployment or hardening work? [Resolved, Data Model Error Handling Disposition]
